title: Why Trust the Bible?
excerpt: Can we still trust the Bible today? A simple look at why God’s Word still matters.
question: Can we still trust the Bible today?
why_it_matters: Every day we hear many voices — social media, opinions, trends, and fears. Some people wonder if the Bible is too old, changed, or no longer important. But in a noisy world, knowing what truth to trust matters more than ever.
truth_simple: The Bible has guided lives for generations because it points people to hope, wisdom, correction, and God’s love. Times change. Cultures change. Technology changes. But truth still matters.

scripture.verse_text: Your word is a lamp to guide my feet and a light for my path.

misunderstanding: Some people believe the Bible is outdated or only for the past. But truth does not expire simply because the world changes.
reflection: What voice shapes your thinking most today — trends, fear, opinions, or God’s Word?
closing_thought: The Bible is not just an old book. It is a light for the path, a guide for the heart, and a foundation for faith.

study_connection.title: Why Can I Trust the Bible?
study_connection.label: Read & Study

title: Communion: Biblical Truth or Church Tradition?
excerpt: Communion is practiced by many believers, but do we understand its meaning, origin, and purpose?
question: Is communion biblical truth or just church tradition?
why_it_matters: Communion is one of the most practiced acts in the church, yet many believers move through it as routine. Understanding its origin helps us return to Christ, not just church habit.
truth_simple: Communion began with Jesus at the Last Supper. He used bread and the cup to point to His body, His blood, His sacrifice, and His love. The method may vary, but the meaning must remain.

scripture.verse_text: Do this in remembrance of Me.

misunderstanding: Some people see communion only as a church tradition or repeated ritual. But communion is rooted in Christ and calls the heart to remember Him with understanding.
reflection: Am I following routine, or am I truly remembering Christ?
closing_thought: Communion is not about performance or appearance. It is a sacred reminder of Christ’s sacrifice, love, and covenant with us.
